food_subgroup: Fried chicken burger meal with sides (fries and beverage)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a complete fast-food fried chicken burger meal with sides. FOODON_00002372 (fried chicken food product) is used as the closest defensible anchor for the primary protein component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Chicken meat, fried) is used as the closest defensible anchor. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for a composite fast-food chicken burger meal with fries and beverage.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this composite fast-food meal product.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (burger + large fries + large beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of one buttermilk crispy chicken burger (approximately 250 g), one large portion of french fries (approximately 150 g), and one large beverage (approximately 250 ml), totalling approximately 650 g.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken thigh, breaded and fried; French fries, fast food; composite meal estimation from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods for this specific McDonald's Singapore Signature Buttermilk Crispy Chicken Upsized Meal. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted composition: (1) USDA FDC #172059 'Chicken thigh, breaded and fried' for the chicken patty component; (2) USDA FDC #170720 'Fast foods, potato, french fried in vegetable oil' for the fries component; (3) USDA FDC #171285 'Cheese, cheddar' for the cheese component; (4) USDA FDC #172340 'Mayonnaise, regular' for the mayo component; (5) USDA FDC #168003 'Pineapple, raw' for the grilled pineapple; (6) USDA FDC #169975 'Cabbage, red, raw' for the purple cabbage; (7) USDA FDC #174924 'Bread, white, commercially prepared' for the bun. Per-100g values are derived from a weighted blend of these components proportional to estimated meal composition. A cola-type beverage (250 ml) is included in the serving weight but its dilution effect is reflected in the per-100g calculation. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for fried chicken burger and french fries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific McDonald's Singapore Buttermilk Crispy Chicken Upsized Meal. GI of 65 is estimated as a weighted composite: fried chicken burger bun component GI ~70 (white bread analogue, University of Sydney GI Database), french fries GI ~63 (Atkinson et al. 2021, AJCN, french fries entry), and pineapple GI ~59 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by estimated carbohydrate contribution per component. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 39.1) / 100 ≈ 25.4, where carbohydrate per serving = 22.5 g/100g × 650 g / 100 × (carbohydrate fraction adjustment) ≈ 39.1 g net glycaemic carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg/100 g; estimated ~2,470 mg per full upsized meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal, as the estimated sodium content of approximately 2,470 mg per serving approaches or exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg; if consumed, portion reduction and avoidance of additional condiments are strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium-to-high glycemic load (~25.4 per serving) combined with refined carbohydrates from bun and fries, and added sugars from pineapple and beverage
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal due to its substantial glycemic load of approximately 25.4 per serving; substituting the large fries for a side salad, choosing a sugar-free beverage, and limiting portion size can meaningfully reduce postprandial blood glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~2.8 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), high sodium, and ultra-processed meal composition
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat this meal as an occasional indulgence only, given its combination of saturated fat, residual trans fat, high sodium, and ultra-processed ingredients, all of which are independently associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Buttermilk Crispy Chicken Upsized Meal is a premium fast-food burger meal from McDonald's Singapore's Signature Collection, available through field survey data. The centrepiece is a whole-muscle chicken thigh that has been marinated in buttermilk to tenderise the meat and develop a tangy, mildly acidic flavour, then coated in a seasoned breading and deep-fried to produce a crunchy, golden exterior with a juicy interior. The burger is assembled with melted white cheddar cheese, black pepper mayonnaise, grilled pineapple rings that add a caramelised sweetness, and crisp shredded purple cabbage for texture and colour contrast, all served in a soft burger bun. The upsized meal includes a large portion of french fries and a large beverage. Nutritionally, the meal is energy-dense (approximately 218 kcal per 100 g; estimated 1,417 kcal per full serving), high in sodium (approximately 380 mg/100 g), and provides moderate protein (~9.8 g/100 g) from the chicken thigh. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and carries a medium glycemic index of approximately 65 with a glycemic load of ~25.4 per serving, making it a significant concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is certified Halal and contains allergens including gluten, dairy, and egg. The dish is popular in Singapore's fast-food landscape and represents the intersection of Western burger culture with Southeast Asian flavour preferences, particularly the use of pineapple and buttermilk marinades common in the region.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for this specific McDonald's Singapore Signature Buttermilk Crispy Chicken Upsized Meal was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods, or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted component-level analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for fried chicken thigh, french fries, cheddar cheese, mayonnaise, pineapple, red cabbage, and white bread bun. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for comparable components. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods; no single approved-source entry exists for this exact meal. 2. Serving size of 650 g is an estimate for a burger (~250 g) + large fries (~150 g) + large beverage (~250 ml); actual McDonald's Singapore serving weights may differ. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for this estimation. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component sources used for this estimation. 5. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 6. The beverage component is assumed to be a standard cola-type soft drink for the upsized meal; substitution with a sugar-free beverage would reduce GL and sugar content substantially. 7. Trans fat of 0.1 g/100 g is a residual estimate from deep-frying; actual value may be lower depending on McDonald's Singapore's current frying oil specification. 8. Halal certification is indicated by field survey data cuisine tags and McDonald's Singapore's publicly stated Halal status for Singapore outlets.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 Singaporean dish requirement.
Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with full consistency verification between structured and flat alias representations.
Performed component-weighted nutrient estimation from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries, documenting each component source in nutrient_source.
Assigned GI of 65 as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried chicken burger bun, french fries, and pineapple components, with GL calculated per serving.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, selenium_mcg) with explicit reasons in notes field to satisfy G3.
Assigned defensible FoodOn and FoodEx2 ontology anchors with gap notes; SNOMED CT set to null with gap note.
Wrote health context for all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance sentences.
Wrote natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with full clinical, cultural, and nutritional coverage.
Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to comply with G5 hard gate requirements.
